qa_pairs.question: An advert featuring a famous painting was placed next to an elevator in an ingenious position. The advert's tagline reads, "Be born again." Who will you become, if just for a moment?
qa_pairs.answer: Adam from Michelangelo's The Creation of Adam.

qa_pairs.question: 'Police [blank] [blank] Was Accidental.' Which two words have been removed from this newspaper headline from a 1993 film?
Putney Bridge in London is unusual in that it has a church at both ends — All Saints on the north bank of the Thames, and St Mary's on the south bank. How did that come about?
qa_pairs.answer: The churches were built before the bridge, when the two sides were separate parishes.

qa_pairs.question: At the 1924 Olympic Games, the American athlete Robert LeGendre set a world record with a long jump of 7.76 metres, which no one surpassed at the event. However, he only received a bronze medal for his efforts. Why?
qa_pairs.answer: Because the long jump was part of the pentathlon, and he finished third overall.

qa_pairs.question: Some time ago, you could try to purchase two items from Rotunden, a Danish supermarket. One would cost you the equivalent of six dollars, while the other cost $150, even though both items were completely identical. Why?
qa_pairs.answer: It was during early 2020 panic-buying: the first bottle of hand sanitizer was normal price, and additional bottles were given a huge price to stop hoarding.

qa_pairs.question: If only more people told the truth, more of these would be sold. What are they?
